name: Portuguese (European)

formality.description: European Portuguese has a more complex T-V system than Brazilian. 'Tu' (informal), 'você' (semi-formal, though considered impolite in some regions), 'o senhor/a senhora' (formal). Portuguese software for Portugal typically uses tu-form or impersonal constructions. 'Você' is less common in Portugal than in Brazil.

gender.inclusiveGuidance: Same rules as Brazilian Portuguese for gender-inclusive writing.

professional.label: Profissional (Professional)
professional.description: Standard for software UI targeting Portugal and European Portuguese speakers.
professional.prompt: Professional European Portuguese. Use tu-form or impersonal constructions (both are standard in Portuguese software for Portugal). European vocabulary — use 'ecrã' not 'tela', 'telemóvel' not 'celular'. Clear, modern language.

notes: European Portuguese has significant vocabulary and grammar differences from Brazilian Portuguese. This is not just a spelling variant.
